content.problemStatement:
You are given two numbers NNN and MMM. A pair of arrays (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) is called KKK-beautiful if and only if:


‚ą£A‚ą£=‚ą£B‚ą£=N|A| = |B| = N‚ą£A‚ą£=‚ą£B‚ą£=N (i.e. both their lengths are equal to NNN)

AAA is lexicographically smaller than BBB
The first index iii where Ai<BiA_i \lt B_iAi‚Äč<Bi‚Äč is KKK
For each KKK (1‚Č§K‚Č§N1 \le K \le N1‚Č§K‚Č§N), find the number of KKK-beautiful pairs and print those values modulo MODMODMOD.

Note: An array XXX is lexicographically smaller than YYY if an only if there exists some iii such that Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi‚Äč<Yi‚Äč and Xj=YjX_j = Y_jXj‚Äč=Yj‚Äč for 1‚Č§j<i1 \le j \lt i1‚Č§j<i. Also, mex(X)mex(X)mex(X) denotes the minimum non-negative integer that does not occur in XXX


Input Format
The first and only line of input contains three integers NNN, MMM, MODMODMOD - the parameters defined in the statement.



Output Format
Output NNN integers such that the iii-th value denotes the number of iii-beautiful pairs modulo MODMODMOD.
